tickets.title: US001: Student questions
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to contact advisor through web interfaces, so that I can ask questions about course registrations.

Acceptance criteria: 
1. Student's question is sent to advisor in < 1 second.
2. Questions won't be deleted for at least 30 days unless advisor want to (by manually deleting it).
3. The system must ensure at least 50 questions are sent to advisor with no question lost at the same time.

tickets.title: US002: Class registration user interface
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to drag and drop classes into a calendar, so that I can easily create an optimal plan.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Drag and drop must update the class in < 1 second.
2. The class after dropped mustn't be added to calendar if its time override other classes time, and warn the student about the override at the same time.
3. The calendar's size must be 50% of the app for any devices (laptops, phones,...)

tickets.title: US003: Student's time boundary
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to set start and end time boundary for my plan which prevent class with time outside of the boundary from being added to my plan, so that I can avoid having classes that I can't attend.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Classes that don't start and end in the boundary musn't be added.
2. The system must be able to handle a plan consists of 50 classes with the delay < 500 miliseconds for any new class added.

tickets.title: US004: Manual system override
tickets.description:
As a Head of Advising, I want to override the system manually if a student has special permission, so that I can handle exceptions when I need to.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The app must ensure that adding the class won't cause data conflicts in database.
2. The database related to the class must be updated even if the class is full using manual system override.
3. The feature can only be used by advisors.
4. The system must log for every manual system override.

tickets.title: US005: Student data protection
tickets.description:
As an IT Security Officer, I want to prevent student from seeing other students' schedule, so that their data is protected.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Student's schedule mustn't be seen by other students.

analysis.ambiguity1: "The course catalog is ugly." is not clear. We don't know why it's ugly and how can we make it better (change color, font, adding user interface,...)
analysis.ambiguity2: "The system needs to stop them from even asking." is ambiguous. How can we know which questions to stop them? Need to clarify better type of whitelisted/ blacklisted questions.
analysis.conflictSolution: Feedback from the Registrar: "the Advising Head said they want students to be able to plan 4 years ahead, even if we do not know if the class is full yet." shows conflict between Head of Advising and Registrar. We can solve this conflict by allowing students to create plans for 4 years ahead, but the courses in their plans must be removed if the planned classes are full.
analysis.nfr1: Security: Student mustn't be able to see another student schedule.
analysis.nfr2: Security: The system must log every time an advisor manually overrides a prerequisite for audit purposes.
analysis.nfr3: Security: Student musn't be able to add class that is full to their roadmap.
